Practical info: Access to effective vector control interventions will need to be maintained in the majority of countries and locations where malaria
control has been effective. This includes settings with ongoing malaria transmission, as well as those in which transmission has
been interrupted but some level of receptivity and importation risk remains. Malaria elimination is defined as the interruption of
local transmission (reduction to zero incidence of indigenous cases) of a specified malaria parasite species in a defined
geographical area as a result of deliberate intervention activities. Following elimination, continued measures to prevent re-
establishment of transmission are usually required
Col2: . Interventions are no longer required once eradication has been achieved.

Practical info: Malaria eradication is defined as the permanent reduction to zero of the worldwide incidence of infection caused by all human
malaria parasite species as a result of deliberate activities.
